_schema: Benchmark run record for subagent collaboration

task.description: Transform GPU monitor into mobile-first SPA with SSE, network access, pull-to-refresh, service worker, and responsive design
task.prompt: lets test the changes by making an update to the GPU monitor to make it more SPA, snappy and both desktop and mobile friendly. also make it so other devices can access by listing on 0.0.0.0

parallel_groups.note: Round 1: parallel bug hunt for 'Illegal invocation' error - both agents independently analyzed the code

quality.notes: Bug immediately identified and fixed. qwen-verifier found the real cause (destructured querySelector losing 'this' binding). gemma-bug-sniffer incorrectly flagged nested requestAnimationFrame (red herring). Fix verified - dashboard now works correctly with SSE, mobile support, and network access.

what_worked: qwen-verifier correctly identified the root cause: destructuring querySelector loses 'this' binding
Parallel diagnostic approach worked well - both agents reviewed different angles
qwen-verifier provided comprehensive SSE review (server-side was clean, bug was frontend)
Fix was simple and targeted - arrow functions preserve 'this' context
SSE implementation working perfectly - real-time updates, no polling needed
Mobile-first responsive design with pull-to-refresh, hamburger menu, touch targets
Network access via 0.0.0.0 with network info modal and copy buttons
Service worker caching for offline/instant loading
what_failed: gemma-bug-sniffer missed the actual bug - focused on nested requestAnimationFrame instead
Initial deployment had critical bug preventing dashboard from loading
Destructuring DOM methods is a common JS gotcha that should be in the style guide

agent_specific_notes.qwen-verifier: Excellent deep dive - correctly identified the destructuring issue and provided comprehensive SSE review. Found the real bug when scout missed it. Essential for complex frontend debugging.
agent_specific_notes.gemma-bug-sniffer: Missed the critical bug this time - flagged nested rAF as the issue (incorrect). Good for surface-level issues but not deep JS binding problems. Consider reserving for simpler bug patterns.

optimization_ideas: Add 'never destructure DOM methods' to project style guide or linting rules
qwen-verifier is the go-to for complex JavaScript bugs involving this/context
gemma-bug-sniffer is better for visual/UI bugs, not deep JS runtime errors
Consider eslint rule 'no-restricted-syntax' to prevent destructuring querySelector
SSE works great - no need to optimize, just add heartbeat for proxy compatibility
